original: Construction of Disneyland commences in Anaheim, California
US Air Force pilot Gail Halvorsen encounters children in at Templehof Airport in Berlin during the Berlin Blockade, giving him the idea to drop candy in 'Operation Little Vittles'
NASA's Apollo 18 and SovietSoyuz 19make 1st US/USSR linkup in space
The Beatles' animated film "Yellow Submarine" premieres in London
Florence Griffith Joynerof the USA sets 100 m woman's record (10.49)
Oldest evidence of bread made from wild grains is discovered by archaeologists in a 14,000-year-old dig in the Black Desert, Jordan

PaleoanthropologistMary Leakeydiscovers the partial skull of a new species of early human ancestor, Zinjanthropus boisei or "Zinj" (now called Paranthropus boisei), which lived in Africa almost 2 million years ago
Robert White in X-15 sets an altitude record of 108 km (354,300 ft)
First two women begin training as FBI agents at Quantico

Cavan Sullivan of the Philadelphia Union becomes the youngest player in a Major League Soccer match and the youngest to debut in any major North American professional sports league at 14 years and 293 days old in a 5-1 win over the New England Revolution at Subaru Park in Chester, Pennsylvania
Scientists solve mystery of sleeping sickness in two villages in northern Kazakhstan - uranium mining had caused increase in carbon monoxide
Don Campbell sets a record for a turbine vehicle at 690.91 kph (429.31 mph)
Douglas "Wrong Way" Corrigan leaves New York flying for Los Angeles and winds up in Ireland supposedly by mistake
A French 4-year-old boy becomes Buddhist Lama Tulku Kalou Rinpoche

Elmer Fudd, originally Egghead, is a Warner Bros. cartoon character created by Tex Avery and Chuck Jones for the Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies series, first debuting as Egghead in "Egghead Rides Again"
Disneyland televises its grand opening in Anaheim, California
Marvel's "Ant-Man", starringPaul Ruddas the titular character, is released
Australian basketball center Liz Cambage scores 53 points as the Dallas Wings beat the NY Liberty 104-87 to set new WNBA single game scoring record
Nils Bohlin, Swedish mechanical engineer and inventor of the three-point seat belt, born in Härnösand, Sweden (d. 2002)
Willie Ito, American animator for Disney Studios ("Lady and the Tramp" spaghetti kiss); Warner Brothers; Hanna-Barbara), born in San Francisco, California
Bob Gibson becomes second pitcher to strike out 3,000 batters (Cesar Geronimo)
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Commerzbank-Arena, Frankfurt, Germany: Japan beats US, 3-1 on penalties; 2-2 after extra time
Graeme Obree breaks the world hour cycling record, riding 51.596km on Old Faithful at the Hamar velodrome in Norway
Georges Lemaître, Belgian Catholic Priest, astronomer and physics professor, who initially proposed the universe expansion and big-bang theories, born in Charleroi, Belgium (d. 1966)
Jimi Hendrix quits as opening act of the Monkees' tour, after playing 7 of a planned 29 shows
"The Mask of Zorro" directed by Martin Campbell, starringAntonio Banderas,Catherine Zeta-JonesandAnthony Hopkinsis released
John Cooper, English race car designer (Cooper Car Company; developed the "Mini"), born in Surbiton, England (d. 2000)
Arco, Idaho, becomes 1st US city lit by nuclear power
1st test flight of US stealth bomber
Humber Estuary Bridge in the UK opens as the world's longest span (1.4 km)
Harvard Observatory takes 1st photograph of a star (Vega)
1st major league game where majority of team is black (Dodgers)
NY Giants future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherCarl Hubbellbegins MLB record 24 game winning streak, beating Pittsburgh Pirates, 6-0 at Forbes Field in Pittsburgh
Soyuz T-12 carries 3 cosmonauts to space station Salyut 7
Matthew Flinderspublishes "A Voyage to Terra Australis," detailing his circumnavigation of Australia, and is the first to name the continent Australia. He dies a day later.
1st ship arrives in Seattle carrying gold from Yukon
2,000 ft long by 1,300 foot wide section of ridge falls into Madis
River Canyon extending man-made Lake Hebgen by 5 miles. (Montana)
Typhoon Talim, makes landfall on China's Guandong coast with winds of nearly 140km/h (87mph), forcing the evacuation of nearly 230,000 people
Ephraim Shay, American inventor of Shay type of geared steam locomotive, born in Huron, Ohio (d. 1916)
Berenice Abbott, American photographer (The World of Atget; Changing New York), born in Springfield, Ohio (d. 1991)
Quino [Joaquín Salvador Lavado Tejón], Argentine cartoonist (Mafalda), born in Mendoza, Argentina (d. 2020)

Peter Schickele (aka P.D.Q. Bach), American bassoonist, Grammy Award-winning composer, classical music parodist (No-No Nonette; Concerto for Horn & Hardart; Silent Running), and broadcaster, born in Ames, Iowa (d. 2024)[1]
Paul Stamets, American mycologist and environmentalist, born in Columbiana, Ohio
Janet Lynn Kavandi, American scientist and NASA astronaut (sk:STS 91), born in Carthage, Missouri
George Frideric Handel's "Water Music" premieres repeatedly on a barge cruising the River Thames in London
Carlos Alberto Torres, Brazilian footballer, Captain of winning World Cup team (1970), born in Rio de Janeiro (d. 2016)
Vince Guaraldi, American jazz pianist and composer ("Cast Your Fate to the Wind"; Peanuts TV specials), born in San Francisco, California (d. 1976)
Battle of Castillon: French army beats English force under John Talbot, turning point of the Hundred Years' War
21st modern Olympic games open in Montreal: 25 African teams (later rising to 33 nations) boycott the games due to New Zealand playing rugby in apartheid South Africa
Former South African PresidentNelson Mandelacalls for commitment by the world to take action against AIDS
ANC leaderNelson Mandela, recently sentenced to life imprisonment, is awarded the Joliot Curie Gold Medal for Peace
Rosa Jackson Lumpkin, lived to be 115 years old, born in Flint, Georgia (d. 1991)
Joe Morello, American jazz drummer (Dave Brubeck Quartet - "Blue Rondo à la Turk"), born in Springfield, Massachussets (d. 2011)
Warwick Hutton, British artist and illustrator of children's books, born in England (d. 1994)
Connie Hawkins, American professional basketball player, 1961-76 ABA MVP-1968 (Harlem Globetrotters, 1963-66; Phoenix Suns, L.A. Lakers), born in Bedford-Stuyvesant, Brooklyn, New York (d. 2017)
Cathy Ferguson, 100m backstroke swimmer (Olympic gold 1964), born in Stockton, California
Phoebe Snow [Laub], American singer-songwriter ("Poetry Man"), born in New York City (d. 2011)
J. Michael Straczynski, American television screenwriter, producer, comic book writer and director (Babylon 5; Jeremiah; Changeling), born in Paterson, New Jersey
Regina Belle, American pop, and R&B singer-songwriter, and Grammy Award winner (with Peabo Bryson - "A Whole New World"), born in Engelwood, New Jersey
